### Both the ;shart_int; and the ;jawab_shart; are ;mahzoof;

Stylistically,
both the ;shart_int; and the ;jawab_shart; may be ;mahzoof;.
For example:


(@) [قَالَتْ بَنَاتُ الْعَمِّ يَا سَلْمَى وَإِنْ كَانَ فَقِيرًا مُعْدَمًا قَالَتْ وَإِنْ]{.ar}
    [modified from [@ibn_hisham_awdah_almasaalik@ 1/42](https://shamela.ws/book/11825/39), cited by @saad_cond@ 219]{.citex}  
    [The uncle's daughters (i.e. cousins) said, "O [#salmA]{.trn2}, even if he is a destitute poor [person]?" She said, "Even if."]{.trans}

In example () above, 
there are two ;j_sh;s.
The first ;j_sh; is
[وَإِنْ كَانَ فَقِيرًا مُعْدَمًا]{.ar}
It's ;jawab_shart; is ;mahzoof;.

The second ;j_sh; is only
[وَإِنْ]{.ar}.
Both its ;shart_int; and ;jawab_shart; are ;mahzoof;.

Here is another example:

(@) [يقول القائل لا آتى الأمير لأنه جائر فيقال ائته وإن]{.ar}
    [[@sirafi_sibawayhi@ 3/264](https://shamela.ws/book/17726/1260), cited by @saad_cond@ 219]{.citex}  
    [A speaker says, "I shall not come to the commander because he is unjust." So it is said (to him), "Come to him, even if."]{.trans}  
    That is: [Come to him, even if he is unjust]{.trans}

<!--
## The deleted ;fi3l_shart;

(@) [التمس ولو خاتما من حديد، واضرب ولو زيدا]{.ar}
    [[[مغني اللبيب]{.ar}](https://ketabonline.com/ar/books/57084/read?part=1&page=266&index=3271127)]{.citex}

## The deleted ;fi3l_jawab_shart;




## [وَإِنْ]{.ar} and [وَلَوْ]{.ar} used for [even if]{.trans}

Wright 2/40A
-->

## A ;shart_int; beginning with a noun

The basic principle for a ;shart_int; is that it should be a ;j_f3l;.
(We include ;kaana; sentences in ;j_f3l;s, loosely speaking.)
And the verb which begins the ;shart_int; is classified as the ;fi3l_shart;.

<!--
We had earlier stated that the ;shart; requires a ;fi3l_shart; in its ;shart_int;.
Based on the preponderance of data in ;samaa3;,
the grammarians extracted a basic principle that the ;shart_int;
containing this ;fi3l_shart; should be a ;j_f3l;.
-->
In all the examples we have seen so far,
this has been the case.
<!--
(We include ;kaana; sentences in ;j_f3l;s, loosely speaking.)
-->
So, in the example:
[إِنْ أَتَيْتَنِي أَكْرَمْتُكَ]{.ar},
the ;shart_int; is the ;j_f3l;
[أَتَيْتَنِي]{.ar},
and it is begun with the ;fi3l_shart; [أَتَى]{.ar}.

Occassionally,
however,
the ;shart_int; is found to begin with a noun.
For example:

(@) [**إِنِ ٱمۡرُؤٌا۟ هَلَكَ** لَیۡسَ لَهُۥ وَلَدࣱ وَلَهُۥۤ أُخۡتࣱ فَلَهَا نِصۡفُ مَا تَرَكَۚ]{.quran}  
    [@https://quran.com/4/176@ cited by [@ibn_ya3ish_mufassal@ 5/120](https://shamela.ws/book/13301/2058)]{.citex}  
    [If a man dies, leaving no child but [only] a sister, she will have half of what he left.]{.trans}  

In example () above, the ;adat_shart; ;in; is directly followed by the noun
[امْرُوٌ]{.ar}
and not the verb
[هَلَكَ]{.ar}.
The way that this,
and similar examples,
are made to align with the above stated principle of a
;shart_int; always being a ;j_f3l;,
is by ;qaddaring; a deleted verb
[هَلَكَ]{.ar}
directly after the ;adat_shart; ;in; as its ;fi3l_shart;, thus^[The following is the standard analysis. Other analyses are described in @abdelghani@ 96--105 and [@radiy_kafiyah@ 4/93--94](https://ketabonline.com/ar/books/23090/read?part=4&page=1531&index=150957/150972)]:

(@) [إِنْ [هَلَكَ] امْرُوٌ]{.ar}

Once the original ;fi3l_shart;
[هَلَكَ]{.ar}
is deleted,
its ;faa3il; is left in place.
And an explanatory verb is added after it which gives the meaning of the deleted verb.
In this, and most cases, the explanatory verb matches the deleted verb.
So the resulting ;shart; becomes
[إِنْ امْرُوٌ هَلَكَ]{.ar}.

In this way, the basic principle of
the ;shart_int; always being a ;j_f3l;
remains intact.
And it restricts the the transformed ;shart;
(after the deletion)
to have a verb.
Therefore, a ;shart; like the following would be disallowed because it does not have a verb:

(@) ;x; [إِنْ عَمْرٌو خَارِجٌ]{.ar}
    [[@ibn_ya3ish_mufassal@ 5/120](https://shamela.ws/book/13301/2058)]{.citex}  
    [If ;3amr; is outside...]{.trans}  

In such ;shart;s where the ;shart_int; begins with a noun,
it is preferred that the ;adat_shart; be ;in;,
and that the ;fi3l_shart; be ;qatala; not ;yaqtul;.^[[@ibn_ya3ish_mufassal@ 5/121](https://shamela.ws/book/13301/2059)]
Nevertheless,
counterexamples do occur.
For example:

(@) [**أَيْنَمَا** الرِّيحُ **تُمَيِّلْهَا** تَمِلْ]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/112](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/966)]{.citex}  
    [Whichever way the wind makes it incline, it inclines.]{.trans}  

(@) [**إِذَا** أَنْتَ لَمْ تَنْفَعْ فَضُرَّ]{.ar}
    [[@ibn_malik_sharh_tasheel@](https://shamela.ws/book/13257/965)]{.citex}  
    [If you cannot benefit then harm.]{.trans}

In example () above,
the ;qaddared; original ;shart; is
[إِذَا [لَمْ تَنْفَعْ]]{.ar}.
The verb
[تَنْفَعْ]{.ar}
(and [لَمْ]{.ar})
is deleted
and its ;faa3il; is left in place.
But because its ;faa3il; is a ;mustatir; pronoun (;qaddared; as [[أَنْتَ]]{.ar}),
it is transformed into its detached pronoun equivalent [أَنْتَ]{.ar} so that it can appear in surface speech.
Then an explanatory verbal expression
[لَمْ تَنْفَعْ]{.ar}
(which matches the deleted verbal expression)
is added,
and we end up with the resultant
[إِذَا أَنْتَ لَمْ تَنْفَعْ]{.ar}.

It is also possible for the explanatorily added verb to not exactly match the ;qaddared; deleted ;fi3l_shart;.
For example:

<!--
Here are some examples:

(@) [إِنْ **أَنْتَ** أَكْرَمْتَ اللَّئِيمَ تَمَرَّدَا]{.ar}
    [abdelghani 96]{.citex}  
    [If you honor an ignoble [person], he rebels]{.trans}  
    (An extra alif is suffixed to [تَمَرَّدَ]{.ar} for rhyme.)

(@) [إذا أنت لم تنفع فضرّ]{.ar}
    [abdelghani 97]{.citex}  
In order to align outlier examples like () and () above with the basic principle
that the ;shart_int; should be a ;j_f3l;,
the grammarians ;qaddar; the ;shart_int; as a ;j_f3l; with a deleted verb.
So the ;taqdir; of the ;shart; for example () above would be:

(@) [إِنْ [أَكْرَمْتَ اللَّئِيم]]{.ar}

The ;shart_int; in the ;taqdir; is
[[أَكْرَمْتَ اللَّئِيم]]{.ar}
which is a ;j_f3l;.
In order to transform this ;taqdir; into the surface speech
[إِنْ أَنْتَ أَكْرَمْتَ اللَّئِيم]{.ar},
the following theoretical steps are taken:

  i. The 
     ;qaddared; ;j_f3l;
     [[أَكْرَمْتَ اللَّئِيم]]{.ar}
     is deleted, except for the ;faa3il; of the ;fi3l_shart;.
 ii. The ;fi3l_shart;'s ;faa3il; is left in place.
     In this case, it is the
     the attached pronoun [تَ]{.ar}.
     But because the 
     an attached pronoun cannot occur by itself,
     it is converted to the detached pronoun
     [أَنْتَ]{.ar}.
iii. An explanatory ;j_f3l;
     [أَكْرَمْتَ اللَّئِيم]{.ar}
     is added after the ;faa3il; [أَنْتَ]{.ar} in order to give the meaning of
     ;qaddared; ;j_f3l;.
     In this (and most) cases, the explanatory ;j_f3l; matches the ;qaddared; original ;shart_int;.
     The verb in the explanatory ;j_f3l; is now the new ;fi3l_shart;.


The benefit of this ;taqdiring;,
and of mandating a ;j_f3l; as the ;shart_int;,
is that it prevents arbitrary ;j_ism;s,
that do not have a verb as the ;fi3l_shart;,
from being the ;shart_int;.
So for example, we cannot say:

(@) ;x; [إِنْ أَنْتَ كَرِيمٌ ...]{.ar}  
    [If you are honorable ...]{.trans}

Let's take a look at another example:
-->

(@) [ فإن أنت لم ينفعك علمك فانتسب ... لعلّك تهديك القرون الأوائل]{.ar}  
    [cited by @abdelghani@ 101; [@ibn_malik_sharh_tasheel@ 2/140](https://shamela.ws/book/13257/536)]{.citex}  
    [If your knowledge does not benefit you then affiliate yourself [to your predecessors]. Perhaps the earlier generations can guide you.]{.trans}

In example () above,
the additional complication is that the ;faa3il; [أَنْتَ]{.ar}
that begins the ;shart_int;, does not match the ;faa3il;
[عِلْمُكَ]{.ar}
of the explanatory ;j_f3l;
[لم ينفعك علمك]{.ar}.^[@abdelghani@ 101--102]
But we can still mold this to fit in with out basic principle and the transformational steps that we gave above. Here's how:

The ;taqdir; for the ;shart; can be ;qaddared; as:

(@) [فإن [لم تنتفع بعلمك]]{.ar}

The ;qaddared; ;j_f3l;
[[لم تنتفع بعلمك]]{.ar}
is deleted except for the ;fi3l_shart; [تنتفع]{.ar}'s ;faa3il;, the ;mustatir; pronoun [[أَنْتَ]]{.ar}.
This ;mustatir; ;faa3il; pronoun
is transformed for surface speech into the detached pronoun [أَنْتَ]{.ar}.
An explanatory ;j_f3l;
[لم ينفعك علمك]{.ar}
is added after the ;faa3il;
[أَنْتَ]{.ar}
to give the meaning of
a
;qaddared; ;j_f3l;.
In this case, the explanatory ;j_f3l; does not exactly match the ;qaddared; original ;shart_int; in wording,
but has a similar meaning.^[[@ibn_malik_sharh_tasheel@ 2/140](https://shamela.ws/book/13257/536); see also [@abu_hayyan_tadhyeel@ 6/313](https://shamela.ws/book/17116/2004)]

The noun which follows the ;adat_shart; may also be in the ;acase; as the ;mafulb; of the ;mahzoof; verb.
For example:

(@) [لا تجزعي إن مُنفِسًا أهلكتُه وإذا هلكتُ فعند ذلك فاجزعي]{.ar}
    [[@ibn_malik_sharh_tasheel@ 2/141](https://shamela.ws/book/13257/537); see also [@abu_hayyan_tadhyeel@ 6/313](https://shamela.ws/book/17116/2004)]{.citex}  
    [Do not grieve if I have destroyed a rival; but if I should perish, then at that time grieve.]{.trans}

Here are some more examples of ;j_sh;s whose ;shart_int; begins with a noun:

(@) [**وَإِنۡ أَحَدࣱ مِّنَ ٱلۡمُشۡرِكِینَ ٱسۡتَجَارَكَ** فَأَجِرۡهُ حَتَّىٰ یَسۡمَعَ كَلَـٰمَ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ أَبۡلِغۡهُ مَأۡمَنَهُۥۚ]{.quran}
    [@https://quran.com/9/6@]{.citex}  
    [And if any one of the polytheists seeks your protection, then grant him protection so that he may hear the words of Allāh [i.e., the Qur’ān].]{.trans}  

(@) [**فَإِنْ أَحَدٌ تَرَخَّصَ** لِقِتَالِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم فِيهَا فَقُولُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ أَذِنَ لِرَسُولِهِ وَلَمْ يَأْذَنْ لَكُمْ]{.ar}
    [@https://sunnah.com/bukhari:104@ cited by @peled_cst@ 20]{.citex}  
    [So if anyone allows himself license because of the fighting of the Messenger of ;Allah; ;pbuh; then say to him, ";Allah; permitted His Messenger and did not permit you."]{.trans}

(@) [**وَإِنْ هُمْ أَبَوْا** فَوَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ، لأُقَاتِلَنَّهُمْ عَلَى أَمْرِي هَذَا حَتَّى تَنْفَرِدَ سَالِفَتِي، وَلَيُنْفِذَنَّ اللَّهُ أَمْرَهُ]{.ar}
    [@https://sunnah.com/bukhari:2731@ cited by @peled_cst@ 19]{.citex}  
    [But if they refuse, then by Him in whose Hand my soul is, I will definitely fight them on this cause of mine until [I die], but ;Allah; will [make His cause victorious].]{.trans}  

Here is an example with ;idha; as the ;adat_shart;:

(@) [إِذَا هُوَ لَمْ يُعْطِ فِيهَا حَقَّهَا تَطَؤُهُ بِأَخْفَافِهَا]{.ar}
    [@https://sunnah.com/bukhari:1402@ cited by @peled_cst@ 27]{.citex}  
    [If he had not given their due [[zakAt]{.trn2}] for them, then they would tread him with their hooves]{.trans}

Here is an example with ;law; as the ;adat_shart;:

(@) [قَالَ أَبُو عُبَيْدَةَ بْنُ الْجَرَّاحِ أَفِرَارًا مِنْ قَدَرِ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ عُمَرُ **لَوْ غَيْرُكَ قَالَهَا** يَا أَبَا عُبَيْدَةَ، نَعَمْ نَفِرُّ مِنْ قَدَرِ اللَّهِ إِلَى قَدَرِ اللَّهِ]{.ar}
    [@https://sunnah.com/bukhari:5729@]{.citex}  
    [[#abU #eubaydah ibn al-#jarrAH]{.trn2} said, "Are you fleeing what ;Allah; has ordained?" [#eumar]{.trn2} said, "**Would that someone else had said [such a thing]**, O [#abU #eubaydah]{.trn2}! Yes, we are fleeing from what Allah has ordained to what Allah has ordained."]{.trans}

<!--
(@) [فإنْ يَكُ عامِرٌ قد قالَ جَهلاً فإنَّ مَظِنَّةَ الجَهْلِ الشّبابُ]{.ar}
    [cited by @gatje_struktur@ 174]{.citex} 
-->



<!--
From the above examples, we can note the following points:

* The ;fi3l_shart; is always ;qatala; (or [;_lam; ;_yaqtul;]{.ar} which is equivalent).
* The original ;faa3il; before the ;fi3l_shart; is usually either a pronoun, or a generic or indefinite noun.
  There very few cases, where it is a definite specific noun.
-->



## The ;j_sh; combined with an ;qasam;

Wright 2/39B

@nahw_arabi@ 2/474

[@ibn_ya3ish_mufassal@ 5/142](https://shamela.ws/book/13301/2080#p1)

@nahw_wafi@ 4/482

;la_in;?

[[لام القسم والموطئة للقسم وجواب القسم]{.ar}](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ghMU0aI1Tpw)

We know from chapter ()
that the ;qasam;,
like the ;shart;,
requires a ;jawab;.
For example,

(@) [وَاللهِ لَأَفْعَلَنَّ]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/104](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/957)]{.citex}  
    [By ;Allah;, I will do it.]{.trans}  

In example () above,
[وَاللهِ]{.ar}
is the ;qasam;, and
[لَأَفْعَلَنَّ]{.ar}
is the ;jawab_qasam;.
The ;jawab_qasam; is
prefixed by the ;jawab_qasam;-;la;.


## The ;jawab_talab;

There is a special kind of sentence where a statement that can be classified as a _;talab;_,
is followed by _;jawab_talab;_.
For example:

(@) [ائْتِنِي آتِكَ]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/93](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/946)]{.citex}  
    [Come to me, I will come to you.]{.trans}

In example () above,
the sentence
[ائْتِنِي]{.ar}
is the ;talab;.
And the sentence
[آتِكَ]{.ar}
is the ;jawab_talab;.

Note that the verb [آتِ]{.ar} in the ;jawab_talab; is in the ;0case;,
as the direct consequence of fulfilling the ;talab;
[ائْتِنِي]{.ar}.
In order to justify the ;0case; of the verb in the ;jawab_talab;,
the grammarians ;qaddar; 
a ;mahzoof; ;shart;
before it
that matches the the ;talab;.
It is this ;mahzoof; ;shart;
that causes the verb in the ;jawab_talab; to be in the ;0case;.^[[@sibawayhi@ 3/94](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/947) citing [الخليل]{.ar}]
Here are a couple of possible ;taqdir;s of example () above:

(@) [ائْتِنِي [إِنْ تَأْتِنِي] آتِكَ]{.ar}  
    [Come to me. [If you come to me], I will come to you.]{.trans}

(@) [ائْتِنِي [إن يكن منك إتيانٌ] آتِكَ]{.ar}  
    [from [@sibawayhi@ 3/94](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/947)]{.citex}  
    [Come to me. [If there is a coming from you], I will come to you.]{.trans}

### Different types of ;talab;s

#;talab;s that take a ;jawab_talab; may be of different types:

#### A ;amr; {.unnumbered}

Example () above is an example of a ;amr; ;talab;.
Here is another example:

(@) [أَسْلِمْ تَسْلَمْ]{.ar}
    [@https://sunnah.com/muslim:1773a@]{.citex}  
    [Submit, you will be in peace.]{.trans}  

A possible ;taqdir; is:

(@) [أَسْلِمْ [إِنْ أَسْلَمْتَ] تَسْلَمْ]{.ar}  
    [Submit. [If you submit] you will be in peace.]{.trans}  

#### A ;nahy; {.unnumbered}

(@) [لا تفعل يكن خيراً لك]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/93](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/946) cited by @saad_cond@ 205]{.citex}  
    [Don't do it, it will be better for you.]{.trans}  

The ;taqdir; for the example above is:

(@) [لا تفعل [إِنْ لَمْ تَفْعَلْ] يكن خيراً لك]{.ar}  
    [Don't do it. [If you don't do it] it will be better for you.]{.trans}  

#### A question {.unnumbered}

Example:

(@) [أَيْنَ بَيْتُكَ أَزُرْكَ]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/94](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/947) cited by @saad_cond@ 206]{.citex}  
    [Where is your house? I will visit you.]{.trans}  

The ;taqdir; for the example above is:

(@) [أَيْنَ بَيْتُكَ [إِنْ أَعْلَمْ مَكَانَ بَيْتِكَ] أَزُرْكَ]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/94](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/947)]{.citex}  
    [Where is your house? [If I know the location of your house] I will visit you.]{.trans} 

Here is another example:

(@) [یَـٰۤأَیُّهَا ٱلَّذِینَ ءَامَنُوا۟ هَلۡ أَدُلُّكُمۡ عَلَىٰ تِجَـٰرَةࣲ تُنجِیكُم مِّنۡ عَذَابٍ أَلِیمࣲ ۝ تُؤۡمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَتُجَـٰهِدُونَ فِی سَبِیلِ ٱللَّهِ بِأَمۡوَ ٰ⁠لِكُمۡ وَأَنفُسِكُمۡۚ ذَ ٰ⁠لِكُمۡ خَیۡرࣱ لَّكُمۡ إِن كُنتُمۡ تَعۡلَمُونَ ۝ **یَغۡفِرۡ** لَكُمۡ ذُنُوبَكُمۡ **وَیُدۡخِلۡكُمۡ** جَنَّـٰتࣲ تَجۡرِی مِن تَحۡتِهَا ٱلۡأَنۡهَـٰرُ وَمَسَـٰكِنَ طَیِّبَةࣰ فِی جَنَّـٰتِ عَدۡنࣲۚ ذَ ٰ⁠لِكَ ٱلۡفَوۡزُ ٱلۡعَظِیمُ ۝ ]{.quran}
    [@https://quran.com/61/10-12@ cited by [@sibawayhi@ 3/94](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/947)]{.citex}  
    [O you who have believed, shall I guide you to a transaction that will save you from a painful punishment?  [It is that] you believe in Allāh and His Messenger and strive in the cause of Allāh with your wealth and your lives. That is best for you, if you only knew.  He will forgive for you your sins and admit you to gardens beneath which rivers flow and pleasant dwellings in gardens of perpetual residence. That is the great attainment.]{.trans}

In example () above, the ;jawab_talab;
[يَغْفِرْ]{.ar} is analyzed to be in the ;0case;
following the question ;talab; [هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ]{.ar}.^[[@sibawayhi@ 3/94](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/947)]

#### A wish {.unnumbered}

Example:

(@) [أَلَا مَاءَ أَشْرَبْهُ]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/93](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/946) cited by @saad_cond@ 206]{.citex}  
    [Is there no water I would drink?]{.trans} 

#;taqdir;:

(@) [أَلَا مَاءَ [إِنْ يَكُنْ مَاءٌ] أَشْرَبْهُ]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/94](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/947)]{.citex}  
    [Is there no water? [If there were water] I would drink it.]{.trans} 

Example:

(@) [لَيْتَهُ عِنْدَنَا يُحَدِّثْنَا]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/93](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/946)]{.citex}  
    [Would that he were with us, he would talk to us.]{.trans}

#;taqdir;:

(@) [لَيْتَهُ عِنْدَنَا [إِنْ يَكُنْ عِنْدَنَا] يُحَدِّثْنَا]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/94](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/947)]{.citex}  
    [Would that he were with us. [If he were with us] he would talk to us.]{.trans}

#### An offer {.unnumbered}

Example:

(@) [أَلَا تَنْزِلُ تُصِبْ خَيْرًا]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/93](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/946)]{.citex}  
    [Won't you come settle? You will obtain benefit.]{.trans}

#;taqdir;:

(@) [أَلَا تَنْزِلُ [إِنْ تَنْزِلْ] تُصِبْ خَيْرًا]{.ar}  
    [Won't you come settle? [If you will come settle] you will obtain benefit.]{.trans}

### The verb following the ;talab; may optionally be in the ;ucase;

In the examples above, the 
verb following the ;talab; may optionally be in the ;ucase;.^[[@sibawayhi@ 3/95](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/948)]
So for example, we can say:

(@) [ائْتِنِي **آتِيكَ**]{.ar}  
    [Come to me. I will come to you.]{.trans}  

<!--
When constructed in this way, the second sentence
(@) [آتيك]{.ar}  
is independent from the first sentence,
and not necessarily its direct consequence.
-->
In such cases, any ;qaddared; ;shart; would not govern the ;ucase; verb.
Here are some more examples:

(@) [خُذۡ مِنۡ أَمۡوَ ٰ⁠لِهِمۡ صَدَقَةࣰ **تُطَهِّرُهُمۡ** وَتُزَكِّیهِم بِهَا]{.quran}
    [@https://quran.com/9/103@ cited by @saad_cond@ 207]{.citex}  
    [Take, [O Muḥammad], from their wealth a charity by which you purify them and cause them increase, and invoke [Allāh's blessings] upon them.]{.trans}

(@) [فَهَبۡ لِی مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِیࣰّا **یَرِثُنِی** وَیَرِثُ مِنۡ ءَالِ یَعۡقُوبَۖ]{.quran}
    [@https://quran.com/19/5-6@ cited by @saad_cond@ 207]{.citex}  
    [so give me from Yourself an heir Who will inherit me and inherit from the family of Jacob.]{.trans}

### The ;qaddared; ;shart; should match the ;talab;

The grammarians specify that the ;jawab_talab; should be the direct consequence
of satisfying a ;qaddared; ;shart; that matches the ;talab;.
Let's explain what we mean by taking a look at the following (non-complying) example:

(@) ;x; [لَا تَدْنُ مِنَ الْأَسَدِ **يَأْكُلْكَ**]{.ar}
    [[@sibawayhi@ 3/97](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/950)]{.citex}  
    [Don't go near the lion, it will eat you.]{.trans}

In example () above,
a ;qaddared; ;shart; that matches the ;talab; is
[إِنْ لَمْ تَدْنُ مِنَ الْأَسَدِ]{.ar}.
If we insert this ;shart; into the original sentence we get:

(@) ;x; [لا تدن من الأسد [إِنْ لَمْ تَدْنُ مِنَ الْأَسَدِ] ياكلك]{.ar}  
    [Don't go near the lion. [If you don't go near the lion] it will eat you.]{.trans}

As you can see, the sentence does not now make logical sense.
In such cases, it is better that the verb following the ;talab; be in the ;ucase;.^[[@sibawayhi@ 3/97](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/950)]
So we should say

(@) ;v; [لَا تَدْنُ مِنَ الْأَسَدِ **يَأْكُلُكَ**]{.ar}  
    [Don't go near the lion. It will eat you.]{.trans}

A ;fa; may also be prefixed to the ;ucase; verb with no problem:

(@) ;v; [لَا تَدْنُ مِنَ الْأَسَدِ **فَيَأْكُلُكَ**]{.ar}  
    [Don't go near the lion. For it will eat you.]{.trans}

By the way, example () [أَلَا تَنْزِلُ تُصِبْ خَيْرًا]{.ar}
is not a problem because
the offer
[أَلَا تَنْزِلُ]{.ar}
is equivalent to the ;amr;
[انْزِلْ]{.ar}.^[[@sibawayhi@ 3/94](https://shamela.ws/book/23018/947)]

Similarly, example () [أَلَا مَاءَ أَشْرَبْهُ]{.ar}
is not a problem because
the wish
[أَلَا مَاءَ]{.ar}
is equivalent to hoping for water
[أَتَمَنَّى مَاءً]{.ar}.
So the ;qaddared; ;shart;
[إِنْ يَكُنْ مَاءٌ]{.ar}
is valid.

## ;3atf; in the ;shart; or the ;jawab_shart;

Wright 2/40CD

@nahw_arabi@ 2/468ff

## Consecutive conditions

@nahw_wafi@ 4/489

also condition followed by question

## ;idha; al-fujaaiyyah

@nahw_arabi@ 2/467

## The ;j_sh; within a larger sentence

@saad_cond@ chapter 6

## Further reading

1. CONDITIONAL SENTENCES WITHIN THE ARAB GRAMMATICAL TRADITION by AHMED ABDEL-AZIM ABDEL-GHANI, Ph.D. thesis, The University of Leeds February 1981.
2. CONDITIONAL STRUCTURE IN CLASSICAL ARABIC: A GENERAL DESCRIPTIVE STUDY, SALMAN AL-SAAD PhD The University of London 2010.
3. [الدلالة الزمنبة للجملة الشرطية في النحو العربي لنور الدين قفي]{.ar}
4. [الجملة الشرطية عند النحاة العرب لأبي أوس إبراهيم الشمسان]{.ar}
5. [آراء وأفكار حول الجملة الشرطية في العربية عبد العليم بوفاتح]{.ar}
6. [التأويل الزمني للجملة الشرطية في اللغة العربية لعارف الدين]{.ar}
7. TRK Alfraidi, Conditional sentences in Modern Written Arabic
8. Helmut Gätje, Zur Struktur gestörter Konditionalgefüge im Arabischen

---

لَئِنْ أَعْطَيْتَنِيهِ لاَ يُخْلَصُ إِلَيْهِمْ أَبَدًا
3110

لَوْ أَنَّ لَكَ مَا فِي الأَرْضِ مِنْ شَىْءٍ كُنْتَ تَفْتَدِي بِهِ
3334

إِنْ كُنْتَ كَاذِبًا فَصَيَّرَكَ اللَّهُ إِلَى مَا كُنْتَ
3464

فَإِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ بِهِ بِأَرْضٍ فَلاَ تَقْدَمُوا عَلَيْهِ وَإِذَا وَقَعَ بِأَرْضٍ وَأَنْتُمْ بِهَا فَلاَ تَخْرُجُوا فِرَارًا مِنْهُ
3473

إِذَا لَمْ تَسْتَحِي فَافْعَلْ مَا شِئْتَ
3482

إِذَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ أَنْتُمْ وَزَيْدُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ فِي شَىْءٍ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ فَاكْتُبُوهُ بِلِسَانِ قُرَيْشٍ
3506

إِنْ كَتَمْتَ عَلَىَّ أَخْبَرْتُكَ
3522

فَإِنْ طَالَتْ بِكَ حَيَاةٌ لَتَرَيَنَّ الظَّعِينَةَ تَرْتَحِلُ مِنَ الْحِيرَةِ حَتَّى تَطُوفَ بِالْكَعْبَةِ
وَلَئِنْ طَالَتْ بِكَ حَيَاةٌ لَتُفْتَحَنَّ كُنُوزُ كِسْرَى
3595

لَوْ سَأَلْتَنِي هَذِهِ الْقِطْعَةَ مَا أَعْطَيْتُكَهَا وَلَنْ تَعْدُوَ أَمْرَ اللَّهِ فِيكَ وَلَئِنْ أَدْبَرْتَ لَيَعْقِرَنَّكَ اللَّهُ
3620

إِنْ كُنْتَ إِنَّمَا اشْتَرَيْتَنِي لِنَفْسِكَ فَأَمْسِكْنِي وَإِنْ كُنْتَ إِنَّمَا اشْتَرَيْتَنِي لِلَّهِ فَدَعْنِي وَعَمَلَ اللَّهِ
3755

قَالَ إِنْ أَعْطَيْتَنِي عَهْدًا وَمِيثَاقًا لَتُرْشِدَنَّنِي فَعَلْتُ
3861

لَوْلاَ أَنَّكَ مَعَ أَبِي صَفْوَانَ مَا رَجَعْتَ إِلَى أَهْلِكَ سَالِمًا. فَقَالَ لَهُ سَعْدٌ وَرَفَعَ صَوْتَهُ عَلَيْهِ أَمَا وَاللَّهِ لَئِنْ مَنَعْتَنِي هَذَا لأَمْنَعَنَّكَ مَا هُوَ أَشَدُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْهُ
3950

إِنْ شِئْتَ أَنْكَحْتُكَ حَفْصَةَ بِنْتَ عُمَرَ
4005

إِنْ رَأَيْتُمُونَا ظَهَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ فَلاَ تَبْرَحُوا
وَإِنْ رَأَيْتُمُوهُمْ ظَهَرُوا عَلَيْنَا فَلاَ تُعِينُونَا
4043

إِنْ قَتَلْتَ حَمْزَةَ بِعَمِّي فَأَنْتَ حُرٌّ
4072

فَقَالُوا لَكُمُ الْعَهْدُ وَالْمِيثَاقُ إِنْ نَزَلْتُمْ إِلَيْنَا أَنْ لاَ نَقْتُلَ مِنْكُمْ رَجُلاً.
4086

وَإِنْ كُنْتَ تُرِيدُ الْمَالَ فَسَلْ مِنْهُ مَا شِئْتَ
4372

فَإِذَا رَأَيْتَ الَّذِينَ يَتَّبِعُونَ مَا تَشَابَهَ مِنْهُ فَأُولَئِكَ الَّذِينَ سَمَّى اللَّهُ فَاحْذَرُوهُمْ
4547

إِنْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً فَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ

إِنْ كُنْتِ بَرِيئَةً فَسَيُبَرِّئُكِ اللَّهُ وَإِنْ كُنْتِ أَلْمَمْتِ بِذَنْبٍ فَاسْتَغْفِرِي اللَّهَ وَتُوبِي إِلَيْهِ
4690

فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُونُوا بَاكِينَ فَلاَ تَدْخُلُوا عَلَيْهِمْ
4702

وَإِنْ تَسْأَلِ الْجَارِيَةَ تَصْدُقْكَ
4750

فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُنْ تَرَاهُ فَإِنَّهُ يَرَاكَ
4777

 فَإِنْ كَانَ لِي عِلْمٌ خَبَّرْتُكَ بِهِ
 4913

إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَكَ بِهَا حَاجَةٌ فَزَوِّجْنِيهَا
إِنْ لَبِسْتَهُ لَمْ يَكُنْ عَلَيْهَا مِنْهُ شَىْءٌ وَإِنْ لَبِسَتْهُ لَمْ يَكُنْ عَلَيْكَ شَىْءٌ
5030

فَإِذَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ فَقُومُوا عَنْهُ
5060

إِذَا أَتَى أَحَدَكُمْ خَادِمُهُ بِطَعَامِهِ فَإِنْ لَمْ يُجْلِسْهُ مَعَهُ فَلْيُنَاوِلْهُ أُكْلَةً أَوْ أُكْلَتَيْنِ أَوْ لُقْمَةً أَوْ لُقْمَتَيْنِ
5460

وَإِنْ وَجَدْتَ مَعَ كَلْبِكَ أَوْ كِلاَبِكَ كَلْبًا غَيْرَهُ فَخَشِيتَ أَنْ يَكُونَ أَخَذَهُ مَعَهُ وَقَدْ قَتَلَهُ فَلاَ تَأْكُلْ فَإِنَّمَا ذَكَرْتَ اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَى كَلْبِكَ وَلَمْ تَذْكُرْهُ عَلَى غَيْرِهِ
5475

وَإِنْ خَالَطَهَا كِلاَبٌ مِنْ غَيْرِهَا فَلاَ تَأْكُلْ
5483

فَإِنْ وَجَدْتُمْ غَيْرَ آنِيَتِهِمْ فَلاَ تَأْكُلُوا فِيهَا، وَإِنْ لَمْ تَجِدُوا فَاغْسِلُوهَا ثُمَّ كُلُوا فِيهَا
5488

فَإِذَا رَأَيْتُمْ مِنْهَا شَيْئًا فَصَلُّوا وَادْعُوا اللَّهَ حَتَّى يَكْشِفَهَا
5785

إِنْ كُنْتَ تَسْمَعُ صَوْتِي لَمَّا جِئْتَ
6140

فَإِنْ مُتَّ مُتَّ عَلَى الْفِطْرَةِ
6313

إِنْ أُوتِيتَهَا عَنْ مَسْأَلَةٍ وُكِلْتَ إِلَيْهَا، وَإِنْ أُوتِيتَهَا مِنْ غَيْرِ مَسْأَلَةٍ أُعِنْتَ عَلَيْهَا
6622

اللَّهُمَّ إِنْ كُنْتَ تَعْلَمُ فِيَّ خَيْرًا فَأَرِنِي رُؤْيَا
7028

إِنْ لَمْ تَجِدِينِي فَأْتِي أَبَا بَكْرٍ
7220
